title: Implement persistent background audio session for main app
description: The main app must stay alive in the background whenever the WhispIt keyboard is enabled so it can respond to Darwin notifications from the keyboard. Strategy: (a) Add 'audio' to UIBackgroundModes in main-app Info.plist, (b) Configure AVAudioSession with category .playAndRecord and .mixWithOthers option, (c) Start a silent audio loop on launch to anchor the session. Ship with a clear explanation in the UI that this is why the red mic indicator is visible. Accept the battery tradeoff. This is the critical wake mechanism — without it, the keyboard cannot reach the main app.

title: Add WhisperKit Swift Package Manager dependency to main app target
description: Add https://github.com/argmaxinc/WhisperKit via Xcode SPM to the WhispItApp target (NOT the keyboard extension — models are too large for a keyboard extension's memory budget). Pin to a recent stable release. Verify a trivial import compiles.

title: Implement Shared/Services/DictionaryManager.swift (CRUD + JSON persistence)
description: Load/save [DictionaryEntry] as JSON in the App Group shared container. API: addEntry, removeEntry, allEntries, entriesMatching(query), incrementFrequency(for word). Must be safe to use from both keyboard extension and main app (file coordination for concurrent access). Used by the keyboard to feed prompt prefixes to WhisperKit and by the main app to display/edit the dictionary.

title: Implement Shared/Services/SettingsManager.swift (App Group UserDefaults wrapper)
description: Wrap UserDefaults(suiteName: appGroupID) with typed accessors for dictation preferences (silenceTimeout, language, cleanupEnabled). Observable via NotificationCenter or Combine so the keyboard can react to main-app setting changes live.

title: Implement Shared/Services/IPCManager.swift (Darwin notifications + shared files)
description: Wrap CFNotificationCenterGetDarwinNotifyCenter for posting/observing cross-process notifications. Provide helpers to write/read JSON payloads to the shared App Group file container. API: post(_ notification:), observe(_ notification:, handler:), writeSharedState(_:), readSharedState() throws. Both keyboard extension and main app use this for all IPC.

title: Define Shared/Models — DictionaryEntry, TranscriptionResult, SharedState
description: Create Codable structs in Shared/Models/: (a) DictionaryEntry { id: UUID, word: String, dateAdded: Date, source: EntrySource (automatic/manual), frequency: Int }, (b) TranscriptionResult { raw: String, cleaned: String, timestamp: Date }, (c) SharedState { recordingActive: Bool, lastInterimTranscript: String, lastError: String? }. Used by both targets for IPC payloads.

title: Define Shared/Constants.swift with App Group ID and Darwin notification names
description: Create Shared/Constants.swift exporting: appGroupID ('group.com.whispit.shared'), Darwin notification names (recordingStartRequested, recordingStopRequested, transcriptionReady, cleanedTextReady), and shared file paths (for audio data, interim transcripts, final cleaned text). Single source of truth for both targets.

title: Create ios/exportOptions.plist for TestFlight export
description: Create ios/exportOptions.plist with method=app-store-connect, teamID=LTU7433MF2, signingStyle=automatic. This file is required by the `xcodebuild -exportArchive` command used during TestFlight uploads (see global CLAUDE.md iOS TestFlight Deployment section).

title: Configure automatic code signing for Taco23 team (LTU7433MF2)
description: Set DEVELOPMENT_TEAM=LTU7433MF2 in project.pbxproj for both app and keyboard targets. Use automatic signing style. Verify the project builds and is deployable to a physical device. Needed for TestFlight uploads later.

title: Configure bundle IDs, iOS 26+ deployment target, and Apple Intelligence capability
description: Set bundle ID to com.whispit.app for the main app and com.whispit.app.keyboard for the extension. Set minimum deployment target to iOS 26.0 (required for Foundation Models framework). Declare Apple Intelligence / Foundation Models capability where required. Verify the project builds on an iPhone 15 Pro simulator/device.

title: Configure App Group entitlements for keyboard↔main-app IPC
description: Add the App Group 'group.com.whispit.shared' to both the main app and keyboard extension entitlements. This App Group is the substrate for all IPC: shared UserDefaults (settings, dictionary), shared file container (audio buffers, cleaned text), and the target of Darwin notifications. Verify read/write works from both targets with a smoke test.

title: Bootstrap Xcode project with main app, keyboard extension, and shared framework targets
description: Create a new Xcode project named WhispIt with three targets: WhispItApp (main iOS app), WhispItKeyboard (custom keyboard extension), and a Shared framework for code used by both. Set deployment target to iOS 26.0. Use Swift 5.9+ and SwiftUI. Configure the folder structure per WhispIt-Spec.md (Shared/, WhispItApp/, WhispItKeyboard/).

title: Error handling and empty states pass across main app + keyboard
description: Sweep all surfaces for error/empty states: Apple Intelligence unavailable (show informative message + fall back to uncleaned transcript), WhisperKit model missing (prompt to re-download), audio session couldn't activate (explain + retry), dictionary empty (helpful empty state with 'add your first word'), transcription returned empty (don't insert, show brief 'no speech detected' feedback in the keyboard banner), Foundation Models call failed (fall back to raw transcript).

title: Configure Info.plist — usage descriptions, ITSAppUsesNonExemptEncryption, background modes
description: Main app Info.plist must include: NSMicrophoneUsageDescription, NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription, NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription (required by linked libraries per global CLAUDE.md even though unused), ITSAppUsesNonExemptEncryption=false (skips compliance prompt), CFBundleIconName=AppIcon, UIBackgroundModes=['audio']. Keyboard-extension Info.plist needs NSExtension.NSExtensionAttributes.RequestsOpenAccess=true and IsASCIICapable=false.

title: Implement DictationPreferencesView — silence timeout only
description: Single preference for v1: silence timeout picker (1s / 2s / 3s / 5s, default 2s) — used by VAD to decide when to auto-stop recording. No auto-punctuation toggle, no language picker, no raw mode (all cut from v1). Stored via SettingsManager.

title: Implement DictionaryView — searchable list with CRUD and source filter
description: List all DictionaryEntry items from DictionaryManager. Search bar filters by prefix. Filter control: All / Automatic / Manual. Each row shows word + source badge + frequency count. Swipe-to-delete on any row. 'Add Word' button opens a text-field sheet for manual entry (source=manual). Automatic entries display an 'automatic' badge.

title: Implement SettingsView (root) with navigation to sub-screens
description: Settings root: navigation rows to Personal Dictionary, Dictation Preferences, About. About row shows version info + a link to the privacy policy. No tone settings (cut from v1).

title: Implement HomeView — minimal status + link to Settings
description: Simple dashboard: shows status ('Ready — type in any app and tap the mic') if everything is configured, or a prompt to finish onboarding if not. Link to Settings. That's it — no stats, no in-app dictation area (both cut from v1).

title: Implement OnboardingView — permissions, model download, keyboard setup
description: Sequential onboarding flow in SwiftUI: (1) welcome + explain persistent-background-audio tradeoff (user will see the red mic indicator — this is expected, processing is fully on-device, nothing leaves the phone), (2) request microphone permission, (3) request speech recognition permission, (4) download WhisperKit model with progress bar (uses model-download service), (5) instructions + deep link to Settings→General→Keyboards to add WhispIt + enable 'Allow Full Access', (6) verify Apple Intelligence is enabled (show instructions pointing to Settings if not), (7) test dictation with a sample sentence. User can't skip forward — each step gates the next.

title: Add haptic feedback for keyboard interactions
description: UIImpactFeedbackGenerator: light impact on mic button press (start recording), medium impact on recording stop, success notification haptic on text insertion. Respect the system haptic-feedback setting (check UIDevice or relevant capability).

title: Insert cleaned text via textDocumentProxy on cleanedTextReady
description: When the keyboard receives the 'cleanedTextReady' Darwin notification, read the cleaned text from the shared file container and call self.textDocumentProxy.insertText(cleanedText). Append a trailing space if natural. Handle edge cases: text field no longer accepting input (user dismissed the keyboard), empty cleaned text (silent failure + log via SharedState.lastError).

title: Wire keyboard mic tap → Darwin notification → main-app recording
description: On mic button tap (idle state): post 'recordingStartRequested' Darwin notification via IPCManager. On mic button tap (recording state): post 'recordingStopRequested'. The main app (kept alive by its background audio session) receives these and drives the audio pipeline. Keyboard also observes SharedState updates and the 'cleanedTextReady' notification to update its own UI.

title: Implement WhispItKeyboard/Views/TranscriptionBanner.swift (live partial transcript)
description: A strip above the keyboard that shows live interim transcription while recording. Reads SharedState.lastInterimTranscript via IPCManager observation (the main app updates this as WhisperKit emits partials). On cleanedTextReady, briefly show the cleaned version before hiding. Auto-hides when idle. Keep it compact — one or two lines with truncation.

title: Implement WhispItKeyboard/Views/MicButton.swift with state animations
description: Mic button with four states: idle (default tint), recording (pulsing red glow), processing (spinner/shimmer with 'Cleaning up…' label), done (brief checkmark flash). Driven by SharedState.recordingActive and a local 'processing' flag set between stop-tap and cleanedTextReady. Smooth SwiftUI animations. Tap toggles start/stop.

title: Implement WhispItKeyboard/Views/KeyboardView.swift (QWERTY layout)
description: SwiftUI QWERTY keyboard: letter rows, shift, backspace, numbers/symbols toggle, space, return, globe (required by Apple for switching keyboards), and a prominent mic button. Support light/dark mode via system appearance. Evaluate using KeyboardKit (github.com/KeyboardKit/KeyboardKit) as a base — if it saves meaningful effort, use it; otherwise implement minimally from scratch. Document the decision in issue notes when you start the work.

title: Set up WhispItKeyboard target with UIInputViewController subclass
description: Create the keyboard extension target. Info.plist must include RequestsOpenAccess=YES (needed for App Group communication). Main class KeyboardViewController: UIInputViewController — hosts the SwiftUI keyboard view via UIHostingController. Keep the extension's memory footprint minimal (target <50MB) — NO WhisperKit, NO Foundation Models, NO audio capture in this target.

title: Integrate personal dictionary into WhisperKit transcription prompt
description: Before each transcription, load all DictionaryEntry words from DictionaryManager and pass them as the initial prompt/prefix to WhisperKit (this biases recognition toward known proper nouns and jargon). Keep the prompt concise — prepend a natural-sounding sentence that uses the vocabulary rather than dumping a raw word list, since Whisper's prompt has a token budget. Document the chosen format in code comments... actually, document it in the bd issue notes, not code comments.

title: Implement WhispItApp/Services/DictionaryLearningService.swift (frequency-based auto-learn)
description: After each successful cleanup, extract candidate proper nouns from the cleaned text (heuristics: capitalized mid-sentence, all-caps 2+ chars, not in a standard English wordlist). For each candidate, increment its frequency counter via DictionaryManager. When a candidate crosses a threshold (e.g. 3 sightings across sessions), auto-add it with source=automatic. NO correction-pair logic — that was cut from v1. Keep this service simple and well-unit-tested because bad auto-adds pollute future WhisperKit prompts.

title: Wire end-to-end pipeline: keyboard signal → audio → raw → cleaned → keyboard
description: Compose AudioCaptureService + TranscriptionService + CleanupService into a single pipeline coordinator in the main app. On Darwin notification 'recordingStartRequested': start capture + streaming transcription, write interim results to SharedState for the keyboard to display live. On 'recordingStopRequested' (or VAD auto-stop): finalize the transcript, run CleanupService, write the cleaned text to the shared file, post 'cleanedTextReady' Darwin notification. Handle errors at every stage and surface them via SharedState.lastError.

title: Handle AVAudioSession interruptions (phone call, Siri, other audio)
description: Observe AVAudioSession.interruptionNotification. On .began, stop any active recording gracefully and update SharedState so the keyboard shows an error/idle state. On .ended with .shouldResume, re-activate the audio session. Also handle route changes (headphones plugged/unplugged) without crashing the audio engine.

title: Implement WhisperKit model download flow with progress reporting
description: On first launch (or if model missing), download whisper-large-v3-turbo via WhisperKit's built-in download API. Emit progress (0.0–1.0) to the onboarding UI. Store in the App Group container so both targets *could* access it (though only the main app will load it). Handle download cancellation/resume and retry on failure.

title: Implement WhispItApp/Services/CleanupService.swift with Foundation Models
description: Wrap Apple's Foundation Models framework (iOS 26+). Use @Generable output type (e.g. struct CleanedTranscript { let text: String }). Single cleanup prompt: 'Clean up this raw voice transcription. Remove filler words (um, uh, like, you know, so, basically). Remove false starts and self-corrections, keeping only the corrected version. Fix grammar and punctuation. Maintain the speaker's intended meaning exactly. Do NOT add, embellish, or change the meaning.' No tone parameter — that was cut from v1. Handle cases where Apple Intelligence is unavailable (fallback: return raw transcript unchanged + surface a warning).

title: Implement Voice Activity Detection (VAD) for auto-stop after silence
description: Monitor audio energy in AudioCaptureService. When sustained silence (RMS below threshold) exceeds the user's configured timeout (default 2s, range 1-5s from DictationPreferences), trigger a 'stop recording' event. Expose as a publisher the main-app pipeline can subscribe to.

title: Implement WhispItApp/Services/TranscriptionService.swift with WhisperKit
description: Wrap WhisperKit. Load whisper-large-v3-turbo (~800MB) on first use, keep resident. API: transcribe(audio: AVAudioPCMBuffer, prompt: String?) → AsyncStream<String> emitting partial transcripts, plus a final(() async throws -> String) for the completed transcript. Accept an optional prompt/prefix parameter so the caller can inject the personal-dictionary vocabulary for better proper-noun recognition. Don't unload the model between dictations — keep it warm.

title: Implement WhispItApp/Services/AudioCaptureService.swift
description: Wrap AVAudioEngine with a tap on the input node. Output audio at 16kHz mono Float32 (WhisperKit's expected input format). API: start(), stop(), and a streaming interface that emits AVAudioPCMBuffer chunks. Handle format conversion from hardware format to 16kHz mono. No transcription here — this service only captures audio.

title: Ship first TestFlight build and add user as internal tester
description: Follow the TestFlight deployment steps in global CLAUDE.md: preflight checks (DEVELOPMENT_TEAM=LTU7433MF2, ios/.xcode.env.local node path, Info.plist flags, icons), archive, export with ios/exportOptions.plist, upload via xcrun altool with API key MLHBANTW2M. Bump CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION as needed. User must have app created in App Store Connect with bundle ID com.whispit.app and be added to an internal TestFlight group. Report TestFlight build number on completion.

title: Write privacy policy (on-device processing, no telemetry)
description: Draft a plain-language privacy policy stating: (1) all voice processing is on-device — audio and transcripts never leave the phone, (2) no analytics or telemetry in v1, (3) the personal dictionary is stored locally in the App Group container, (4) the persistent red-mic indicator is due to the background audio session required for keyboard-to-main-app IPC and does NOT mean we're listening. Host it somewhere (GitHub Pages or a simple static page) and link from the About screen.

title: Add app icons (1024x1024 + all required sizes) and launch screen
description: Generate app icon assets for all required iOS sizes in WhispItApp/Resources/Assets.xcassets/AppIcon.appiconset. A 1024x1024 icon is required or the TestFlight upload fails (see global CLAUDE.md). Also create a simple launch screen. Use a placeholder icon for now — just something that unblocks TestFlight.
